skillName: Generate geometric sequences using term to term rules
misconceptionSummary: Students swap multiplicative and additive thinking when generating a geometric sequence.

lines.text: Start at 5, multiply by 2 each time

steps.narration: The second step should also use multiplication by two.
Now every step follows the multiplier rule.

lines.text: The wrong answer switched to adding 5

steps.narration: The student accidentally used an additive pattern instead of the given multiplicative rule.

visuals.expression: Use the stated multiplier every time
visuals.label: Do not replace a geometric rule with an additive one

steps.narration: To avoid the mistake, write the operation next to each step as you generate the sequence.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: turning a multiply-by rule into an add-by rule. Geometric generation must preserve the multiplier.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: If the rule says multiply, keep multiplying at every step.
